text:
Once upon a moonlit night, in a quaint village shrouded in mist, there lived an old owl named Oliver. Known for his wisdom and twinkling eyes, Oliver seldom left his tall oak tree home.

One evening, as the stars twinkled above, Oliver heard a wee voice. It didn't sound owl-like at all—Ill look? he thought alarmingly.

Intrigued, he croaked down. A tiny figure hooted back, not owl-like at all but distinctly... human! Oliver blinked. Had his eyes grown dim?

"Lost?" Oliver croaked.

"Yes," replied a young girl with curls like golden wheat. "These shoes—I can't take them off. I'm searching for my lost cat."

"I'll help." Oliver scratched his fluffy chin thoughtfully. "You look... quite distraught."

"I am!" She beamed happily. "Your feathers look... wise."

Oliver chuckled. "Well, I've never had much cause for worry
